SondePolishR 0.0.9000

Apply a drift correction to a parameter

Source: R/shift-points.R
apply_drift_shift.Rd

Applies a linear correction to data based on the final "corrected" value often determined via a freshly calibrated sonde compared to the sonde that's been deployed ("uncorrected").

Usage

apply_drift_shift(x, rows, corrected, uncorrected)

Arguments

x

Vector of data to apply correction to.

rows

Row numbers of the values within x that should be corrected.

corrected

Value of the corrected end value, used to determine how much to shift data.

uncorrected

Value of the uncorrected value, used to determine how much to shift data.

Value

`x` with the drift shift applied.

Examples

rows <- example_data$FileName == "example-data1.csv"
x_shift <- apply_drift_shift(example_data$fDOM_QSU, rows, 17.49, 21.71)
